Plaintiff Tianyi Lu'S Motion For Leave To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendant Maven Commercial Inc. (Continued From 4/18/2022 On The Court'S Own Motion)
Off Calendar Apr-19-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Plaintiff Tianyi Lu's Motion For Leave To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendant Maven Commercial Inc.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is DENIED. Plaintiff has not shown a "substantial probability" of proving punitive damages. Civil Code 3294, 3295. The events in Plaintiff's declaration are not so far outside the ordinary experience of tenants that there is a substantial probability a jury will find malice, oppression, or fraud. The landlord may be able to rebut Plaintiff's showings. Re the foul smells and cigarette smoke, the lease warned that not all tenants were subject to the smoking ban and that there are many bars and restaurants nearby, which may contribute to smells; also Plaintiff had a pet, which a jury may find relevant. Re behavior by another tenant, there is insufficient information about what he did that I can conclude a jury will find it is outside the ordinary experience of dealing with neighbors. Re the failure to clean up bike parts and respond to mail theft, the landlord submitted evidence of regular cleaning. Re cockroaches, the landlord hired an exterminator after being notified. Based on this record, it is as plausible that a jury will find a constructive eviction as that it will find for the defense. This is inconsistent with a "substantial probability" of proving punitive damages.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the pro tem judge at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)
